title: RNA extraction from hairy roots of common bean and cDNA synthesis
author: Ronal Pacheco
materials: Macerate root tissue using liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Load 100 mg of macerated tissue into a 1
steps.instruction: Load 100 mg of macerated tissue into a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube and add 1 mL of TRIzol Reagent Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #15596026 . If the amount of root tissue is less than 100 mg, add the equivalent amount of Trizol.

steps.title: Mix by vortexing 00:00:15 and incubate...
steps.instruction: Mix by vortexing 00:00:15 and incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge 11800 rpm, 4 °C, 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 11800 rpm, 4 °C, 15 minutes

steps.title: Add 200 µL, chloroform:isoamyl alcohol 24:1...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, chloroform:isoamyl alcohol 24:1 , mix by vortexing 00:00:15 and incubate for 00:03:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous phase to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous phase to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of isopropanol, mix...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of isopropanol, mix by immersion, and incubate Overnight -20 °C . The original protocol indicates incubating for 10 min at room temperature; however, we have had a low RNA concentration using these conditions. We strongly recommend incubating for at least 6 h at -20°C to get a higher concentration of RNA.

steps.title: Centrifuge 11800 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 11800 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous phase to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous phase to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of 4 M...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of 4 M LiCl and rise the pellet, do not resuspend, vortex slowly. LiCl increased the RNA concentration; thereby, this is an important step to reaching a high concentration of RNA.

steps.title: Centrifuge 5900 rpm, 4 °C, 00:20:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5900 rpm, 4 °C, 00:20:00

steps.title: Discard the LiCl phase
steps.instruction: Discard the LiCl phase. LiCl is difficult to remove; so, try to remove all remanents using a micropipette or syringe.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of tris-EDTA buffer pH 8
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of tris-EDTA buffer pH 8 . Resuspend RNA by vortexing.

steps.title: Add 500 µL, chloroform:isoamyl alcohol 24:1...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL, chloroform:isoamyl alcohol 24:1 v/v and mix by vortexing.

steps.title: Centrifuge 5900 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5900 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous phase to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous phase to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of isopropanol and...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of isopropanol and 66 µL of 3 M sodium acetate pH 5.2 Mix by immersion and incubate Overnight -20 °C The original protocol does not include an overnight incubation step, but we strongly recommend incubating for at least 6 h at -20°C to get a higher concentration of RNA.

steps.title: Centrifuge 11800 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 11800 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes

steps.title: Discard the aqueous phase and vacuum...
steps.instruction: Discard the aqueous phase and vacuum or air dry the RNA pellet.

steps.title: Resuspend RNA pellets using nuclease-free water
steps.instruction: Resuspend RNA pellets using nuclease-free water. Preferably, use DEPC-treated water.

steps.title: Check the integrity of RNA in...
steps.instruction: Check the integrity of RNA in a 1% agarose gel treated with bleach. Aranda PS, LaJoie DM, Jorcyk CL. .

steps.title: Prepare a dilution (1/10) of each...
steps.instruction: Prepare a dilution (1/10) of each RNA sample and quantify the concentration using a NanoDrop™ 2000c Spectrophotometer Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #ND-2000C or an equivalent instrument.

steps.title: Prepare one aliquot 10 µL of...
steps.instruction: Prepare one aliquot 10 µL of each RNA sample at 10 ng/μl

steps.title: Add 1 µL of DNase I...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of DNase I recombinant RNase-free Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #04716728001 and 1 µL of the corresponding buffer (10X) to each RNA sample. To reduce pipetting errors, prepare a mix of the DNase and the incubation buffer 1:1 v/v (total volume according to the number of samples) and add 2 µL of this mix to each RNA sample.

steps.title: Incubate samples 00:30:00 37 °C After...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples 00:30:00 37 °C After this step, a qPCR assay should be performed to check if traces of genomic DNA are remaining. According to our experience, DNase I recombinant RNase-free Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #04716728001 efficiently remove all traces of genomic DNA by performing this step.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of 10 millimolar...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of 10 millimolar (mM), oligo (dT) to each sample and incubate 00:05:00 70 °C , then immediately incubate On ice, 3 m .

steps.title: Prepare a mix containing 1 µL...
steps.instruction: Prepare a mix containing 1 µL of RevertAid Reverse Transcriptase (200 U/µL) Thermo Fisher Catalog #EP0442 , 4 µL of the corresponding buffer (5X), and 2 µL, dNTP mix . Add 7 µL of the mix to each RNA sample.

steps.title: Store cDNA samples at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Store cDNA samples at -20 °C .
